name: E2E: every tool that is actually installed in the environment runs through the runner to a terminal verdict (clean, findings, or not_enforced) and never a usage error — proving the adapter wiring
command: TD="$(mktemp -d)"; printf 'print(1)\n' > "$TD/s.py"; bad=0; ran=0; while IFS='|' read -r tool bin; do [ -z "$tool" ] && continue; command -v "$bin" >/dev/null 2>&1 || continue; ran=$((ran+1)); timeout 25 bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/run-tool.sh" --tool "$tool" --file "$TD/s.py" --json >/dev/null 2>/dev/null; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && bad=$((bad+1)); done < <(node -e 'const m=JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(process.argv[1],"utf8"));process.stdout.write(m.tools.map(x=>x.tool+"|"+(x.bin||x.tool)).join("\n"))' "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/toolchain.json"); rm -rf "$TD"; [ "$bad" -eq 0 ] && echo present-tools-run-sane ran=$ran 1>&2
